failingTest: `getTagDef('PROSODY')` returns `undefined` instead of the prosody definition; `getTagDef('Break')` returns `undefined` instead of the break definition (case-sensitive comparison bug).
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — make getTagDef() comparison case-insensitive
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase/mixed-case lookup test cases
fixApproach: In `getTagDef()` (tags.ts), normalize both the lookup key and each tag's name to lowercase before comparing: change `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`. This automatically fixes `isTagSupportedByProvider()` since it calls `getTagDef()`. Then add test cases in tags.test.ts covering uppercase (`'PROSODY'`), mixed-case (`'Break'`), already-lowercase (`'emphasis'`), and a nonexistent tag (`'nonexistent'`) to confirm the fix.
steps: Change the tag-name comparison from `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`.

Open `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts` and add test cases: `getTagDef('PROSODY')` returns prosody def, `getTagDef('Break')` returns break def, `getTagDef('emphasis')` (already lowercase) still returns emphasis def, `getTagDef('nonexistent')` returns undefined, and `isTagSupportedByProvider('PROSODY', provider)` equals `isTagSupportedByProvider('prosody', provider)`.
Run `npm run test` and confirm all tests pass.
summary: Make `getTagDef()` case-insensitive so SSML tag lookups work regardless of casing (e.g. `PROSODY`, `Break`, `prosody` all resolve).
